enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

Who are the singers of the song Adada Adada?

The song is sung by Shreya Ghoshal and Sriram Parthasarathy.

Who composed the music for the movie Veera Sivaji?

The music was composed by the talented D. Imman.

What is the theme of the song Adada Adada?

The song beautifully portrays romantic emotions and the joy of love.

Who are the lead actors in the movie Veera Sivaji?

The movie stars Vikram Prabhu and Shamili in lead roles.

When was the movie Veera Sivaji released?

The movie was released in the year 2016.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Adada Adada from the movie Veera Sivaji, released in 2016, features the lead pair Vikram Prabhu and Shamili. Sung by Shreya Ghoshal and Sriram Parthasarathy, the song is a romantic melody composed by D. Imman with lyrics by Yugabharathi. It captures the essence of love and emotional connection, making it a favorite for romantic occasions. The soothing tune and heartfelt lyrics evoke a sense of warmth and affection, resonating deeply with listeners.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Adada Adada, a mesmerizing melody from the movie Veera Sivaji, showcases the harmonious blend of Shreya Ghoshal and Sriram Parthasarathy's vocals. Composed by the talented D. Imman, this song captures the essence of romance and emotional connection. Its soothing tune and heartfelt lyrics by Yugabharathi make it a timeless addition to Tamil cinema's musical treasures.
stanzaMeanings: Adada Adada Song Lyrics Veera Sivaji Singer:Sriram Parthasarathy,Shreya Ghoshal Music : D. Imman Lyrics : Yugabharathi
Oh my, oh my, a goddess like you has never been seen by my eyes until now.
The moments when we both share our desires, is this it?
In your immense beauty, I dissolve completely, bit by bit.
My love, you are the greatest purpose of my existence.
Oh my, oh my, a goddess like you has never been seen by my eyes until now.
Morning and evening, we will walk hand in hand.
We will talk and pass the time, smiling and resting in each other's embrace.
Half of me is you, half of you is me; together we understand the rest.
We will work together and forget to sleep or rest.
Closing our eyes, we will search for ourselves.
We will run like children under the moonlit streets.
We will love without separation, without knowing what separation means.
